questions.text: How can we stop the services in android?
options: By using the stopSelf() and stopService() method
questions.correctAnswer: By using the stopSelf() and stopService() method
questions.explanation: In Android, you stop services using stopSelf() from within the service or stopService() from outside it.

questions.text: What is an activity in android?
options: A single screen in an application with supporting java code
questions.correctAnswer: A single screen in an application with supporting java code
questions.explanation: An Activity is a screen that users see and interact with in an Android app.

questions.text: Which term best describes 'a program that duplicates the look and feel of a particular device'?
questions.explanation: An emulator simulates the appearance and functionality of a real device on a computer.

questions.text: Which of the following is the first callback method that is invoked by the system during an activity life-cycle?
questions.explanation: onCreate() is the first method called when an activity is created.

questions.text: What is the use of content provider in Android?
options: For storing the data in the database
For sharing the data between applications
For sending the data from an application to another application
questions.correctAnswer: For sharing the data between applications
questions.explanation: Content providers manage access to a structured set of data and enable data sharing between apps.

questions.text: Which of the following is contained in the src folder?
questions.explanation: The src folder in an Android project contains the Java source code files.

questions.text: Which of the following method is used to handle what happens after clicking a button?
questions.explanation: The onClick method handles actions performed after a button is clicked.

questions.text: Which of the following android component displays the part of an activity on screen?
questions.explanation: A Fragment represents a portion of the user interface in an activity.
